chunk_summary: This section outlines performance thresholds and monitoring metrics for managing malnutrition in pediatric outpatient and facility-based care, emphasizing recovery, defaulter, and death rates, as well as OPD checklist protocols for program evaluation and corrective action.
learning_objectives: Understand performance thresholds for pediatric malnutrition programs, including recovery, defaulter, and death rates.
Learn how to use OPD checklists and anthropometric data for monitoring and evaluation.
Identify corrective actions for high defaulter or death rates in malnutrition programs.
Analyze outpatient monitoring workflows and referral triggers for nonresponse cases.
key_facts: Recovery rate should be above 75%, defaulter rate below 15%, and death rate below 5%.
Average weight gain during rehabilitation should be at least 8 g/kg/day; flag if lower.
A defaulter is defined as a child absent for three consecutive days without discharge.
Nonresponse triggers include failure to gain weight for two weeks or nonrecovery after three months.
Rapid OPD assessments include anthropometry, diet recall, and Anganwadi food consumption tracking.
reference_quote: Acceptable program thresholds used for service monitoring include recovery rate above 75%, death rate below 5%, defaulter rate below 15%, and average weight gain during rehabilitation at or above about 8 g/kg/day.
document_key_topics: Monitoring and evaluation metrics for outpatient and facility care
Corrective actions for high defaulter or death rates
OPD checklist and rapid assessment protocols
